title: Cyclosorus × pseudoliukiuensis
intro: Cyclosorus × pseudoliukiuensis, a fern species native to tropical regions, is a type of vascular plant characterized by its distinctive fronds and ability to thrive in humid environments. Traditionally, Cyclosorus × pseudoliukiuensis has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit therapeutic activities in in vitro and in vivo studies. In herbal medicine, Cyclosorus × pseudoliukiuensis is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and its extracts have been used in the production of dietary supplements and traditional herbal remedies.

Cyclosorus × pseudoliukiuensis is a hybrid of Cyclosorus spp., which is part of the Pteridaceae family. This family is a group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of the Pteridaceae family is within the division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The Pteridophyta division is further classified under the kingdom Plantae.
